transcript: [sample from KISS' 1976 Destroyer Tour Toronto show] Alright, Toronto!
Alright! So, we’re here in Toronto and, uh, we’re here with Randy and friends.
Alright! [delay, pitch-shifted] Thank you, Randy and friends, for bein’ here. Thank ya so much! [delay, pitch-shifted]
I sure do appreciate your time! Aw, yeah!
[sample from KISS' 1976 Destroyer Tour Toronto show] Alright, Toronto! Listen!
[sample from KISS' 1976 Destroyer Tour Toronto show] You know, it wasn’t too long ago we was here and I tell ya, every time we come here—
I have a question: what’s your favorite food?
[sample from KISS' 1976 Destroyer Tour Toronto show] damnit, it just gets better and better!
Can you guys dig that or…?—
And what—? What’s—? And what’s your favorite color, man?
Orange! I’d say orange! [delay, pitch-shifted] Yeah, probably orange up ‘ere.
[sample of Wayne Bent ] I swear it!
What’s your favorite color up ‘ere?! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Do you have a color, anybody?
''[crowd members shout over each other, many shout, “Green!”]
Green. Everybody is all about the green—
Well, I mean, we do have that in common with Colorado.
Dynamite! Thank yall for bein’ here! Can and do appreciate ya!—
Oh, alright! We can have a little fun tonight.
You getting some nipples tonight on the horn?
Yeah, we’re gonna call some numbers, sure thing! Uh-huh! [delay, pitch, shifted]
If anyone has any questions for Longmont, don’t be shy. We have numbers. We wanna know… [to “Man(1)”] Do you have a question?
Hi! So, uh, Longmont Potion Castle, my question for you is this: Uh—
—In the Adult Swim—in the Adult Swim Off the Air segment that your, ah, calls [unclear] on, there’s this illustration of a cat with the face of a man and he’s wearing sunglasses. He’s—

—Oh, yeah! Yeah, that’s a good observation. I’ve seen that! I know what you’re talkin’ about. [delay, pitch-shifted] I don’t know where it came from, what the origin of that is. There’s a new one coming out, uh, this month. It’s about fashion. It’s, uh, Off the Air: Fashion. It’s a brand new one and they’re gonna have me in there. So, it’ll be about the pleated looks and so on.

That cat face, then, is still gonna be there? ‘Cause the reason I bring this up is, the other day, I was in this bar and I saw in the back of this club, right behind the bar, re-pasted to the wall, that same man in sunglasses and cat ears. And I asked the bartender about it and they said they didn’t know. And it was the most mysterious photo of my entire life. [unclear]
So keep an eye out there, man. [unclear]. Thank you.
Well, thank you! That’s a good question. Very mysterious, very oblique! Nebulous!
This is Tim. What are ya doin’ up ‘ere?

Uh, Tim Phillips. Listen, I—I’m new in the area. I was wonderin’, can I, uh, can I tap into your septic tank, uh, little bit later? I got some structural issues here at home. I got my own hose and everything.
Well why don’t you tap into your mom?
Hey, guy, it won’t take long. It’ll just be for a few minutes and I’ll take care of everything. Just tap right in to your tank, is that okay with you up ‘ere?
Can you tap into your mother?
[exhales] Listen, guy, and I don’t want ya messin’ with my hose. I’ll find out about it if you mess with my hose, okay? Just to be clear.
You mean your mother and your wife are hoes? [laughs]
Listen, guy, I need your tank!—
Oh, my god! Don’t you have a life?
A wife? you just insulted her, tough guy, and I’m gonna pay ya back! [exhales]
—Thank you and thank me and I thank everybody! It’s a pleasant night! Stars’re shinin’ bright! [exhales]
Alright, so we got some questions. Let’s go [unclear]
Um, how do you feel about anonymity in, like, what you’re doing and at what point of your—in your—what you’re doing [unclear] decide on being completely anonymous?
Anonymity. What made you decide to be anonymous and how do you feel about it?

I was about 16, goin’ on 17 when, uh, I started recording, you know? And I was sittin’ there, um, in my bedroom at home and I was puttin’ the cassette cover together. [sniffs] Sittin’ there and thought, “What am I gonna do with this?” And, uh, at the last second before I went and made the copies, I put “Various Artists Compilation” on there just to be easier on myself. I didn’t wanna answer a buncha questions about it, so I put “Various Artisrs”. And that saved me ’til maybe the next album. Volume 2, I put “Various Artists” again and people started to be like, “Well, what do you mean, ‘Various Artists’?” You know, and I cut my own voice out, a lot, too, you might remember just because of that. I had myself in there somewhat but a lot of times I didn’t. And then by #3—Volume 3—Why, I just stopped doin’ that and just started to, uh, kinda own up to it, if you will. So it was a lie, is what it was, you know? Uh—
—I do what I can, do what I can, do what I can! [exhales] Oh, yeah!
—Bry— Hey, this is Bryan. How’re ya doin’?
Okay. Why are you calling me?
I’m calling you because, uh, I got some structural issues here at my house. I need to tap into your septic tank for a little bit here, tonight. That be okay? I got my own hose and everything.
Wait a minute, wait a minute, who are you trying—? I still don’t know wha—where—? Who you calling me from?
I’m calling you. I’m local and I need your help there, big guy—
—Yeah? What kind of help?! What are you talkin’ about?
I need to set my hose into your septic tank. I got problems here at home—big problems, here, on my structural issues, here!
I live near you! So I’m gonna—
—bring my hose over and we’re gonna set it up; does that sound good?
Of course not! It’s trouble for me! What are you talkin’ about?! And I still don’t know who the hell you are!
Why is this such a big deal? It’s—you know—I—I’m gonna do it. It’ll be fine. I’ll pay the—
Yeah but where—where do you live?!—
I’m gonna come over there and I’m gonna get what I need! [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
You not gonna get any because I’m gonna call the police on you—on you right now! Okay?—
—No, don’t do that! Don’t do that. I just need a little help—
—Then don’t—don’t [unclear] talk to me like that! Okay?! You don’t get the hose! I’m Jewish and [unclear] it’s trouble for me! And I won’t give it to you any other time! I got no b—business with you!
Don’t mess with my hose. ‘Cause I’ll find out about it if you do! [delay, pitch-shifted] Just don’t get in ma way!
Okay, you guys. Sorry about that! [sniffs]
He’s got some, uh, plumbing to deal with here. I’m kinda multitasking, if you haven’t noticed. So—
[laughs] That was! Very good, very good!—
[sample from KISS' 1976 Destroyer Tour Toronto show] Alright, Toronto!—
Uh, we got another number, uh…
We got another number dialin’ here. Oh! [delay, pitch-shifted]
—Listen, I got my hose ready and I’ll—I’m just on my way right now. I’m walkin’ over there right now, okay?
You try and come here and you’ll see what’s gonna happen to you.
—Ya mothafucka! Stop it! You motherfucker criminal!
Don’t you worry about a thing. [laughs] Uh! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Yeah! Is there any, uh, tricks and tips you can offer to, uh, customer service [unclear, perhaps, “heathens”] to sort of summon—summon the patience? On the horn, there?

[inhales] I’ve done a lotta customer service in my time. I mean, that’s kinda why I do the UPS thing with people, ‘cause it’s plausible. It has a grain of plausibility. Even when you’re tryin’ ta deliver goofy loaf or whatever the case may be, you know, people’ll hang in. And it’ll buy ya some time, is what it does. It buys you some time. You know what I mean?
Yeah! Kinda like, ah, you’re runnin’ out the clock on both ends, you know? Runnin’ out your shift and—
[laughs] Yeah! [delay, pitch-shifted] [sniffs] So that’s something. That helps. And, uh, yeah and obviously not cursing at them, at least not right off the bat.
[samples of Wayne Bent ] And having sex was God’s instructions— It wasn’t at my instruction—
So, you know, those are some tips just a little bit, little sumpin’, little tidbit up ‘ere! Thank you for asking—
—Okay. Okay, it’s ringing. Will! Great, okay.
[sample of Wayne Bent disciple] Well, he is the Messiah. He is, uh, uh— To me he is Christ returned in spirit.
Hey. Not too much. This is Bo.
Bo. I’m new to the area.

What did you say up ‘ere?! [delay, pitch-shifted] What’re you sayin’ up ‘ere?! No, I’m—I’m—I’m Bo. [clears throat] Bobo. [delay, pitch-shifted, layered] I’m Bobo. Yeah, I’m Bobo. I’m Bobo [delay, pitch-shifted]. And, uh, I need to, uh, get with you about your hose. I wanted to borrow your hose, if I could. Would that work or…? I gotta let you go. I do. I just do!
So that guy knew somethin’ that was up right off the bat! Maybe I shoulda gone into UPS, huh? Followed my own advice up ‘ere, you know what I’m sayin?
Let’s try the singer of Fucked Up. Okay, it’s, uh, 6—Canadian 647—
Hey, this is Bo. I’m new to the area up here.

I’m ro—I’m just catty corner to ya. Listen, I need a favor. I wanted just to tap into your, uh, septic tank here, tonight. I got some structural issues here at home. Just for a few minutes. I got my own hose and everything, okay?
Whadda ya need? [exhales] Whadda ya need me to do, bring ya a ladder or somethin’?—
—I don’t have a septic tank! I don’t have anything like that!
O’ course ya do, o’ course ya do! I already went over there and looked at it, ya know? I hope you don’t mind, but I was there earlier today, okay?
I don’t have a septic tank.
Oh, ya sure do. I saw it and I’m—
—I’m gonna be right there and I’m gonna hose it up. I just wanna make sure, ya know, that ya don’t be surprised when I get there, okay?
Police! ‘Cause I don’t know what you—why you’d be on my property.
’Cause I told ya. I need to set my hose up. Now look, jus—
Just stay outta my way, okay? I don’t need the police and I don’t need you nosin’ around, okay? Just keep outta my affairs!
Well, keep outta my o—my house! My property.
As long as you stay outta my way, we should be in good shape.
Well lemme get your name and everything. Go on, now—
[samples of Wayne Bent ] Yes. Yes— I swear it!
[sample of Wayne Bent ] I swear it!
Bill! Hey, Bill, what the frick ya doin’ up ‘ere?! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Later! Okay, you said somethin’ about a number up ‘ere, didn’t ye? [exhales]
This is Bo. Can ya hear me?

—Okay, good, listen, I’m on my way over there. I’m kinda new to the area. I got your number at the supermarket. Okay, I just need to tap into your septic tank tonight for a little bit. Alright? I got my own hose.
[breaks up] see you on my lawn with [breaks up]
Hey, you’re cuttin’ out. But I’ll be there in just about 10 minutes. I got some problems with my foundation so I’m just gonna tap into you guys for a bit, okay?
Okay! I—I’m gonna be out on my lawn [breaks up] and—
—Okay, just do me a favor, don’t mess with my hose, alright? I’ll find out about it if you do, so… Okay?—
—[laughs] [breaks up] see you with your hose I’m gonna lasso [breaks up] so hard [breaks up] that hose right up—! [breaks up]
I can’t even hear ya, lady! You’re cuttin’ in ’n out. Why don’t you speak clearly, okay?—
—I— [breaks up] I’m gonna lasso you with that hose right up your—! [breaks up]
Oh, ya are are ya? Well why—I’m gonna do-si-do right on up ‘ere and get with ya a little bit—mix things up a little bit; how’s that grab ya?
Listen, you got a terrible reception. I’m gonna let ya go but I’ll be there with my hose and just—just stay outta my affairs. It’ll be much easier, okay?

[breaks up] I’m a full-stack web developer now! And I just graduated from my coding bootcamp! I’m at the graduation party! And I don’t have time for these kinda shenanigans! If you show up on my lawn with your hose, I’m gonna send 20 bootcamp graduates after you and they’re gonna code your ass so hard you’re never gonna walk again!
Oh, don’t do that! Don’t do that!—
Leave me be! Alright, young lady? Just leave me be!
—Hello? Hello? Hello? Can ya hold on right now? Your mother’s being strapped to the, uh, donkey. She’s gettin’ fucked! Hold on, hold on!—

[samples of Wayne Bent ] Yes. Yes— Well, um— One day, by myself, I sat down in my chair, just relaxing, and then God said to me, “You are Messiah”— It just occured— I swear it by the One who lives forever and ever!— Um, I stood up and suddenly I was forced down on the floor and all I could do was groan— And I started seeing that I was going to have to— Have sex— And having sex was God’s instructions— Do what God tells you!

Okay, so we let her go. Thank you! Thank you! Thank you very much, Royal Theatre! That was funny! She had a terrible connection, but, uh, it was pretty good! Thank you for that one. She a cheerleader or somethin’? I don’t know.
Oh. Well I dialed the wrong number, then—
Hi, this is Bo. How’re ya doin’?
Yeah, good! Hey, uh, I got your number up at the supermarket; I hope that’s okay. I’m new to the area here.

Uh, at the A&P and, uh, hey, guy, I just need to, uh, tap into your septic tank tonight. I got some structural issues here at home, okay? It’ll just for a couple hours. I got my own hose and everything like that. So if you see me out in front, I just wanted to let ya know what I’ll be doing, okay?
And I’ll tap in and— Just, listen, don’t touch the hose while it’s workin, okay?
Okay. ‘Cause I’ll find out about it if ya so, so might as well just leave it alone—
—I know! I know ya know. Okay? Hey, and, uh, can you may—make me some toast—maybe bring that out front? I’m a little hungry. Would that be okay?
I will—I will get you the tuna sandwich. How’s that?—
Okay, touch yourself. See ya later.
Well he was a cooperative sort—
Bless you! You guys rule! Have a fantastic night!
Lotta fun. Have a lotta fun! Time! Have a fantastic night! Have a phantasm! Have a phantasm. Thank you.
